stories.title: Smart Recurrence Picker
stories.description:
Replace the free-text custom recurrence input with a structured picker that validates input, provides live feedback, and powers actual recurrence logic on task completion. Based on patterns from Todoist, Things 3, Apple Reminders, and Google Calendar.

## Storage Format

Canonical structured strings stored in `recurrence_rule TEXT`:
- `every:N:days` — e.g., `every:3:days`
- `every:N:weeks:mon,wed,fri` — weekly with specific days (lowercase 3-letter abbreviations)
- `every:N:months:15` — monthly on specific day (1-31)
- `every:N:months:3rd:tue` — monthly on ordinal weekday (1st/2nd/3rd/4th/last + day)
- `every:N:years:3:30` — yearly on specific month (1-12) + day (1-31)
- After-completion mode: prefix `every!:` instead of `every:` (e.g., `every!:3:days`)
- Optional end date suffix: `|until:YYYY-MM-DD` (e.g., `every:2:weeks:mon,wed|until:2026-06-01`)
- `null` = no recurrence

## One-Time Migration

Add a database migration that sets ALL existing `recurrence_rule` values to `null`. Clean slate — old format (`daily`, `weekly`, `monthly`, `every:3 days`) is not worth migrating.

## Shared Utility: `src/shared/recurrenceUtils.ts`

Create a shared utility with these functions:
- `parseRecurrence(rule: string | null): RecurrenceConfig | null` — parse canonical string to structured object
- `serializeRecurrence(config: RecurrenceConfig): string` — structured object to canonical string
- `describeRecurrence(rule: string | null): string` — human-readable description (e.g., "Every 2 weeks on Mon, Wed")
- `getNextOccurrence(rule: string, fromDate: Date): Date | null` — compute next occurrence date. For Fixed mode: compute from `fromDate`. For After-completion mode: compute from `fromDate` (which will be the completion date). Returns null if end date has passed.
- `isValidRecurrence(rule: string | null): boolean` — validate canonical format

RecurrenceConfig interface:
```typescript
interface RecurrenceConfig {
  interval: number // 1+
  unit: 'days' | 'weeks' | 'months' | 'years'
  weekDays?: string[] // for weeks: ['mon', 'wed', 'fri']
  monthDay?: number // for months: 1-31
  monthOrdinal?: { nth: '1st' | '2nd' | '3rd' | '4th' | 'last'; day: string } // for months: ordinal weekday
  yearMonth?: number // for years: 1-12
  yearDay?: number // for years: 1-31
  afterCompletion: boolean
  untilDate?: string // ISO date YYYY-MM-DD
}
```

## Detail Panel Picker UI

Replace the current `DetailRecurrence.tsx` with a structured inline picker that expands vertically when any preset or Custom is clicked.

**Preset buttons (top row):** None, Daily, Weekly, Monthly — same style as current. Clicking a preset expands the picker pre-filled:
- Daily → `Every 1 days`
- Weekly → `Every 1 weeks` with today's weekday toggled
- Monthly → `Every 1 months` on today's date
- None → clears recurrence, collapses picker

**Picker rows (shown when expanded):**

Row 1: `Every [number input] [unit dropdown]`
- Number input: min 1, arrow up/down to increment/decrement, direct typing allowed
- Unit dropdown: days, weeks, months, years

Row 2 (conditional on unit):
- **weeks**: 7 day-of-week toggle buttons (M T W T F S S) — at least one must be selected. Default: today's weekday. Use compact toggle buttons matching the app's badge/chip style.
- **months**: Two-option toggle — "On day [number input 1-31]" OR "On the [ordinal dropdown: 1st/2nd/3rd/4th/last] [weekday dropdown: Mon-Sun]". Default: "On day [today's date]".
- **years**: Month dropdown (Jan-Dec) + day number input (1-31). Default: today's month and day.
- **days**: no Row 2

Row 3: Segmented toggle — "Fixed" / "After completion". Default: Fixed. Use compact segmented control matching the app's button style.

Row 4: `Ends: [dropdown: Never / On date]`. When "On date" selected, show the existing DatePicker component inline. Default: Never.

**Preview line (always visible when picker is expanded):**
- Below all rows, accent-colored text: `→ Every 2 weeks on Mon, Wed (next: Apr 14)`
- Updates live as any control changes
- "next" date computed from today using `getNextOccurrence`
- If after-completion mode: show `→ Every 3 days after completion (next: computed from today)`

**Keyboard navigation:**
- Tab cycles through picker controls: number → unit → day toggles/month input → mode toggle → end date
- Arrow Left/Right within day toggles moves focus between days, Space toggles
- Arrow Up/Down on number inputs increments/decrements
- Escape collapses the picker

**Auto-set due date:** When a recurrence rule is applied to a task that has no due_date, auto-set `due_date` to today (ISO 8601 date string, no time).

## Task Completion → Clone Logic

In `TaskRepository` (main process), add a `completeRecurringTask(taskId: string)` method that runs in a single transaction:

1. Load the task and verify it has a `recurrence_rule`
2. Parse the rule with `parseRecurrence`
3. Compute next due date:
   - Fixed mode: `getNextOccurrence(rule, task.due_date)` — from the original due date
   - After-completion mode: `getNextOccurrence(rule, new Date())` — from now
4. If `untilDate` is set and next date > untilDate: do NOT create clone, return null
5. Create a new task cloning: title, description, priority, labels (via task_labels), project_id, recurrence_rule, reference_url
6. Set the clone's due_date to the computed next occurrence
7. Set the clone's status to the project's default status (first non-done status by order_index)
8. Copy all subtasks, resetting their status to the project's default status and clearing completed_date
9. Return the new task ID and due date

The renderer must detect when a task with recurrence is moved to a done status. In the task store's status-change logic, after updating the task, check if it has a `recurrence_rule`. If so, call the new `completeRecurringTask` IPC method. On success, show a toast: "Recurring task created → due [formatted date]". Clicking the toast navigates to the new task (select it, switch to its project if needed, open detail panel).

**Add IPC handler:** `task:complete-recurring` that calls `TaskRepository.completeRecurringTask`.
**Add preload bridge method:** `completeRecurringTask(taskId: string): Promise<{ id: string; dueDate: string } | null>`

## Task Row Indicator

In `TaskRow.tsx`, when a task has a non-null `recurrence_rule`, show a small repeat icon (use `Repeat` from lucide-react, 12px, stroke 1.5, text-muted) next to the due date area. On hover, show a tooltip with the human-readable rule from `describeRecurrence`. If there's no due date displayed, show the icon after the task title metadata area.

## Context Menu Updates

In `ContextMenuSubmenus.tsx`, update the `RecurrenceSubmenu`:
- Keep presets: None, Daily, Weekly, Monthly
- Daily sets `every:1:days`
- Weekly sets `every:1:weeks:{today's weekday}` (e.g., `every:1:weeks:mon`)
- Monthly sets `every:1:months:{today's date}` (e.g., `every:1:months:30`)
- Remove the old "Every 3 Days" and "Every 14 Days" presets
- Add a divider, then "Custom..." item at the bottom
- "Custom..." opens the detail panel focused on the recurrence field (field index 6)

## MCP Server Updates

Update the `set_task_recurrence` tool description in `mcp-server.ts` to document the new canonical format. Accept canonical format strings only. Example in description: `every:2:weeks:mon,wed`, `every!:1:months:15|until:2026-12-31`.

## Files to Create
- `src/shared/recurrenceUtils.ts` — parsing, serialization, description, next-date computation
- `src/shared/recurrenceUtils.test.ts` — comprehensive tests for all utility functions

## Files to Modify
- `src/shared/types.ts` — add RecurrenceConfig interface
- `src/renderer/src/features/detail/DetailRecurrence.tsx` — replace with structured picker
- `src/renderer/src/features/detail/DetailPanel.tsx` — wire up auto-set due date on recurrence change
- `src/renderer/src/features/tasks/TaskRow.tsx` — add recurrence indicator icon with tooltip
- `src/renderer/src/shared/components/ContextMenuSubmenus.tsx` — update RecurrenceSubmenu
- `src/renderer/src/shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` — wire Custom... to open detail panel
- `src/main/repositories/TaskRepository.ts` — add completeRecurringTask method
- `src/main/ipc-handlers.ts` — add task:complete-recurring handler
- `src/preload/index.ts` and `src/preload/index.d.ts` — add completeRecurringTask bridge method
- `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/taskStore.ts` — detect done status + recurrence, call IPC, show toast
- `src/main/mcp-server.ts` — update set_task_recurrence tool description
- `src/main/database/migrations.ts` — add migration to clear all existing recurrence_rule values

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking a preset (Daily/Weekly/Monthly) expands the picker pre-filled with that interval and today as the reference day
Number input increments/decrements with arrow keys, minimum value 1
Unit dropdown offers days/weeks/months/years
Selecting weeks shows 7 day-of-week toggle buttons; at least one must be selected
Selecting months shows toggle between 'On day [N]' (1-31) and 'On the [ordinal] [weekday]'
Selecting years shows month dropdown + day input defaulting to today
Fixed / After completion toggle is visible and defaults to Fixed
Ends row shows Never by default; switching to On date shows a date picker
Live preview line updates on every change showing human-readable rule + next occurrence date
Preview uses accent color for valid rules
Clicking None clears recurrence and collapses the picker
Escape collapses the picker
Completing a recurring task creates a new cloned task with next due date and reset subtasks
Clone is not created if the end date has passed
Toast notification appears on clone creation showing next due date
Clicking the toast navigates to the new task
Task row shows a repeat icon for recurring tasks with tooltip showing the rule
Context menu presets use smart defaults (today's weekday/date); Custom... opens detail panel recurrence field
Setting recurrence on a task without a due date auto-sets due date to today
MCP set_task_recurrence accepts the new canonical format
One-time migration clears all existing recurrence_rule values to null
recurrenceUtils.ts has full test coverage for parsing, describing, and next-date computation
All keyboard navigation works: Tab between controls, arrows within, Space to toggle, Escape to collapse
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
